programmes.show_description: Alice gets her claws into dog-lover William's cover after he betrayed his furry friends in ink. Sketch helps Jules, who is branded with the palm-print of her ex.
Five comics create masks that look and taste great, and clear up a mess of their own making, in the showdown to end all showdowns. But who will claim the crown? (Ep2/2)
A chance to watch top moments from the comedy game-show mash-up, with guests including Richard Ayoade, Aisling Bea, Alan Carr, David Mitchell, Katherine Ryan and Johnny Vegas.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Steven Newman, CEO of Loehmann's department stores, goes undercover on the front line of his company, to ensure the outlets are implementing his plans to reinvigorate the brand.
Jane Grote Abell, the chairwoman of Donatos Pizza and daughter of the company's founder, goes undercover to ensure that her father's values are still being implemented.
Essex and east London. Spencer and Sara serve a simple Jewish feast. Lauren and Jack's menu reflects their South African and Burmese heritage. Paul and Ed's food challenges a picky palate.
There are silly dances, jokes and accents, plus plenty of pranks, as couples put their hosting skills to the test in Hertfordshire, in a week featuring Asian, Anglo-Indian and Irish food.
...Selling: Jonie was delighted when her daughter Taisha moved back in. But now there's a struggle for space. To sell their home, Jonathan and Drew must make it look brand new.
The One with Nessie: Calvin the over-enthusiastic groom plans a monster Scottish wedding at Loch Ness for him and his bride-to-be, Leanne.
The Day of the Dead One: Fiery bride Jenna is in for a scary surprise as her fiancé Nathan is planning a Mexican Day of the Dead themed wedding for her.
Joe DiDomizio, the president and CEO of a travel retailer that operates news stands, bookshops and cafés in transport terminals, goes incognito among his own employees.
Greg Miller, CEO of the Larry H. Miller Group, and co-owner of National Basketball Association team the Utah Jazz, dons a disguise to go undercover among his staff.
Town's Inn (Part 1/2): Gordon's at Harpers Ferry in West Virginia to visit The Town's Inn, a historic hotel that's so full of clutter it can barely accommodate guests.
Town's Inn (Part 2/2): Gordon continues his battle to help turn around The Town's Inn in West Virginia. Can he win over the stubborn owner and give the hotel a chance of success?
...and Selling: Brand new series: Nick and Julie are looking for a spacious home to accommodate every generation. But first, can the bros mellow the yellow in their tiny place?
All Work and No Rosé: A primary charter guest gets an unpleasant surprise that threatens to ruin the entire trip. Danni's sent on a difficult mission.
Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack, Mike Wozniak and Sarah Kendall catch rats and have a smashing time delivering crockery on a scooter.
Katherine Ryan hosts a special episode celebrating 100 years of women's suffrage. Roisin Conaty and Jessica Hynes take on Sara Pascoe and Lolly Adefope. With Morgana Robinson.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Michelle Wolf take on Jon Richardson and Rob Beckett. Spencer Jones joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Rachel Riley looks after the numbers.
Dream Job: Jeremy tries to wow Toni with his new job in a music studio, while Mark gets passed over for promotion in favour of Sophie, and goes slowly round the twist.
Funeral: Death and terminal illness prove to be the keys to romantic success for Mark and Jeremy...
Sketch helps Sian cover a clown tattoo that's scaring the life out of anyone who sees it. Jay has to deal with Emma's extremely intimate cat portrait.
Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack, Mike Wozniak and Sarah Kendall catch rats and have a smashing time delivering crockery on a scooter.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Michelle Wolf take on Jon Richardson and Rob Beckett. Spencer Jones joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Rachel Riley looks after the numbers.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
In Stoke-on-Trent Phil's a BBQ pro. Can the newbie hosts find their flow? Gary and Simon attempt to do prawn. But it's the third time this week, will the guests simply yawn? #WorldPoetryDay
Who'll walk away with the grand in Crewe? One couple impress with their Georgian house. But after the pairs give their review. Who will be crowned the best cooking spouse? #WorldPoetryDay
Nathan loves music and his home town, but will his plans make his bride frown? He's got his theme, it's Northern Soul. But can Beccie cope with no control? #WorldPoetryDay
Hopeless romantic Gavin proposed on a hill, but was it all that much of a thrill? He's hoping to pull off a Thai-themed wedding, but is Somerset the ideal setting? #WorldPoetryDay
Time for bride Lauren to be in the dark, as groom Courtney sets their wedding at a park. But not just any park will do, this one's filled with caravans too! #WorldPoetryDay
Are Jas and Bobby heading for a scuffle, in this Titanic-style wedding kerfuffle? Bobby really wants to set sail; but does Jas think his plans are a fail? #WorldPoetryDay
Groom Micky may well be love-struck, but is he maybe pushing his luck? While Sophia wants a wedding that's grand, Micky's plans are more winter wonderland. #WorldPoetryDay
Stephen's undercover in Diamond Resorts International. But is his disguise a wee bit irrational? Before he begins the company probe, he decides he should pierce an ear lobe. #WorldPoetryDay
In PostNet we go undercover. But what will their CEO uncover? It's time for co-founder Steve, to see what they can achieve. But post-recession, can they recover? #WorldPoetryDay
Gordon Ramsay takes on Town's Inn. Where they've apparently never heard of a bin. There's hygiene issues. Please give them some tissues. And then the cleaning can begin. #WorldPoetryDay
Welcome to Lakeview Hotel, Lake Chelan; where owner Brent is without a plan. The restaurant and parlour are seriously failing. Cue Gordon Ramsay, will he be availing? #WorldPoetryDay
Verindar runs her place like a tyrant. Her employees are fed up with staying silent. So Gordon tries to sprinkle his magic. Hopefully the hotel won't stay so tragic. #WorldPoetryDay
If you're looking for a place to sleep, at this resort you won't get a peep. In these not-so-great digs, Brian hosts late-night gigs. And his service would make you weep. #WorldPoetryDay
Gordon visits a huge castle, where the scenery certainly dazzles. But sadly this citadel's no fairy tale; it's Hotel Hell on a massive scale. #WorldPoetryDay
Welcome to the comedy countdown quiz. With our resident wordsmith and maths whizz. Katherine Parkinson and Judi Love are guests. Putting their countdown skills to the test. #WorldPoetryDay
...Christmas Special: Let's look back on Christmas Day. When Jon teams up with Daisy May. Judi and Rob are agog. Joe brings the eggnog. And Jimmy rides in a sleigh. #WorldPoetryDay
How to enhance a one-man show? Add inventiveness and a musical flow. Well, we've got the comedy key. He's weird, he's wonderful, he's Bill Bailey #WorldPoetryDay
